sweep_evidence.reason: no fixes applied yet at this stamp

phase3_findings.description: Cutover doc states the pin inventory backwards: claims 'presence pin, and three assert_pin_red_under behavioral-fix pins' but HEAD carries 1 assert_pin_red_under and 2 assert_pin_unique. The same diff added the run.sh comment documenting that demotion, so the diff self-contradicts.

Changeset claims --self-check 'enforces the provenance, so a synthesizer that stopped stamping it fails loudly rather than silently'. do_self_check is warn-only and returns 0 unconditionally, and its jq is a key-set difference, so a regression to [] / not-run keeps every key present and passes silently.

Three surviving 5-field enumerations of the synthesized record contradict the changeset's 'reconciled across every surface' claim: fixing.md:149 (same paragraph the diff edits), error-handling.md, implement/phases/phase-3-review.md.

ITER_SYNTH_EXPECTED_FIELDS is self-certifying: no divergence guard, and 'synthesized' appears in no schema block so no LR-style comparand exists. Every test derives its expectation from the constant, so a silently dropped member passes by construction.

The loop_role #170 assert_eq can pass vacuously: both operands are derived through sed/tr and an empty extraction yields assert_eq '' '' which passes, retiring the single-source guard. The PR added exactly this positive control for the sibling synth extraction 1,300 lines earlier.

Schema block reference_reads example omits the mandatory reason key, and encodes JSON null as the string 'null' in the outcome enum. sweep_evidence example uses status 'run', a value no writer spec defines (specs define not-run / unrunnable).

The three unrec() reason assertions check only non-emptiness, so a regression collapsing all three onto one shared $what keeps every assertion green while the record stops saying which evidence was unrecoverable.
LR_CONDITIONAL_FIELDS presence pins assert membership but not conditionality: nothing asserts each excluded field is genuinely absent from ITER_EXPECTED_FIELDS.
